name: using-neon
description: Guides and best practices for working with Neon Serverless Postgres. Covers getting started, local development with Neon, choosing a connection method, Neon features, authentication (@neondatabase/auth), PostgREST-style data API (@neondatabase/neon-js), Neon CLI, and Neon's Platform API/SDKs. Use for any Neon-related questions.
capabilities: Guide users through Neon Serverless Postgres setup and configuration
Help choose the right connection method for different platforms and runtimes
Explain Neon features like branching, autoscaling, and scale-to-zero
Provide references to official Neon documentation for verification
Assist with Neon authentication and API integration
Support developer tools like Neon CLI, VSCode extension, and MCP server
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 29 static findings are false positives. This is a documentation skill that provides references to Neon Serverless Postgres documentation. The scanner misinterpreted example curl commands in markdown code blocks as shell execution, hardcoded URLs as potential exfiltration, and metadata text as cryptographic algorithms. No actual security risks present.
valueStatement: Neon is a serverless Postgres platform that separates compute and storage. This skill provides guides and best practices for connecting, managing, and scaling Neon databases in your applications.
limitations: Does not execute database queries or manage actual Neon projects
Does not provide live troubleshooting for Neon service outages
Cannot access user-specific Neon account information or project data
Does not generate code that creates security vulnerabilities

useCases.targetUser: Developers new to Neon Serverless Postgres
useCases.title: New Developer Setting Up Database
useCases.description: A developer new to Neon wants to set up their first serverless Postgres database. The skill guides them through creating a project, getting connection strings, and choosing the right driver.

useCases.title: Choosing Connection Method
useCases.description: A developer building a serverless application needs help selecting between the Neon serverless driver, traditional Postgres drivers, or ORM integrations based on their platform.

useCases.targetUser: Development teams using advanced Neon features
useCases.title: Using Advanced Features
useCases.description: A team wants to leverage Neon features like database branching for development workflows or autoscaling for production. The skill provides context and documentation links.

promptTemplates.title: Getting Started with Neon
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to set up a Neon Serverless Postgres database for my project. What are the first steps?
promptTemplates.scenario: New user wants to understand basic setup process

promptTemplates.title: Connect to Neon from Application
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I connect my [language/framework] application to Neon Serverless Postgres?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer needs connection guidance for specific technology

promptTemplates.title: Choose Connection Method
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the best way to connect to Neon from a serverless function?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer building serverless application needs guidance

promptTemplates.title: Use Database Branching
promptTemplates.prompt: How does Neon database branching work and when should I use it?
promptTemplates.scenario: Team wants to leverage branching for development workflows

bestPractices: Always use the Neon serverless driver for serverless functions and edge runtimes to avoid connection limits
Reference official Neon documentation for the most up-to-date connection strings and API information
Use environment variables for connection strings rather than hardcoding credentials
antiPatterns: Do not assume Neon's connection pooling behavior matches traditional Postgres - it uses HTTP/WebSocket in serverless
Do not guess documentation URLs - use the llms.txt index to find correct pages
Do not make claims about Neon pricing or limits without checking current documentation

faq.question: What is Neon Serverless Postgres?
faq.answer: Neon is a serverless Postgres platform that separates compute and storage. It offers autoscaling, database branching, instant restore, and scale-to-zero capabilities while remaining fully compatible with Postgres.

faq.question: How do I connect to Neon from my application?
faq.answer: Use the @neondatabase/serverless driver for serverless environments, or traditional Postgres drivers like pg for long-running processes. The skill can help you choose the right method for your platform.

faq.question: What is database branching in Neon?
faq.answer: Database branching creates an instant clone of your database. This is useful for development workflows, testing, and CI/CD pipelines where you need isolated database copies.

faq.question: Does Neon work with my ORM?
faq.answer: Yes, Neon works with any ORM that supports Postgres, including Drizzle ORM, Prisma, Sequelize, and SQLAlchemy. The skill includes specific guidance for Drizzle integration.

faq.question: Can I use Neon with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is designed to help Claude Code assist with Neon Serverless Postgres setup and management. It provides guidance for database operations within Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What are Neon's scaling capabilities?
faq.answer: Neon offers automatic scale-to-zero (compute suspends when idle) and autoscaling (compute scales based on workload). You can configure these settings in your Neon project dashboard.
